html:
[1210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不飲 - Literally 'not drink,' used here to describe a person who abstains from alcohol.
  • 燕會 - A formal gathering or banquet, often for social or official purposes.
  • 濡唇 - Literally 'wet the lips,' used here to indicate drinking alcohol.
  • 挟诈 - Literally 'holding deceit,' meaning to be insincere or dishonest.
  • 阴伺 - Literally 'secretly observe,' meaning to spy or watch for someone's mistakes.
  • 售诸长官 - Literally 'sell to the superior officials,' meaning to report someone's mistakes to their superiors.
  • 资进身计 - Literally 'use as a means to advance oneself,' meaning to use someone's mistakes for personal gain.
  • 独醒 - Literally 'alone awake,' a metaphor for being the only one who remains sober or aware.
  • 袒衣示之背 - Literally 'unbuttoned his clothes to show his back,' a gesture of revealing something important.
  • 两瘢 - Literally 'two scars,' referring to marks on the body.
  • 受徒刑 - Literally 'received punishment of the law,' referring to legal punishment.
  • 冥冥不知人 - Literally 'in a daze, unaware of others,' describing a state of unconsciousness or confusion.
  • 据案 - Literally 'sit at a desk,' referring to a judge or official in a courtroom.
  • 诘 - Literally 'question,' meaning to interrogate or ask.
  • 不应坐某罪 - Literally 'should not be punished for a certain crime,' meaning that someone should not be held responsible for a particular offense.
  • 辄断之 - Literally 'arbitrarily decide,' meaning to make a decision without proper justification.
  • 公牍 - Literally 'official documents,' referring to legal or administrative papers.
  • 恨无由取至尔 - Literally 'regret that there is no way to bring them here,' expressing frustration at not being able to access certain documents.
  • 主者 - Literally 'the person in charge,' referring to the judge or official.
  • 左右 - Literally 'those around,' referring to assistants or attendants.
  • 卒趍而出 - Literally 'a soldier rushed out,' describing a quick action.
  • 反复阅视 - Literally 'repeatedly read and examine,' meaning to carefully review documents.
  • 几误杀汝 - Literally 'almost killed you by mistake,' expressing relief at avoiding a serious error.
  • 遣汝归 - Literally 'send you back,' meaning to release someone.
  • 元追吏 - Literally 'the original pursuing official,' referring to the person who initially brought the case.
  • 贤 - Literally 'virtuous,' meaning kind or considerate.
  • 款语 - Literally 'friendly conversation,' meaning to speak in a friendly manner.
  • 力戒 - Literally 'strongly warn,' meaning to give a strong warning.
  • 切勿饮酒 - Literally 'do not drink alcohol,' a direct instruction.
  • 不肯言 - Literally 'refused to say,' indicating reluctance to explain.
  • 腥血交流 - Literally 'blood and gore mixed together,' describing a scene of injury or violence.
  • 疮已溃 - Literally 'the wound has broken open,' indicating the severity of the injury.
  • 愈 - Literally 'recover,' meaning to heal or get better.
  • 性本好饮 - Literally 'nature loves drinking,' describing a person's inherent preference for alcohol.
  • 思冥吏之戒 - Literally 'think of the warning from the underworld official,' referring to the advice given during the near-death experience.
  • 不忍再速死也 - Literally 'cannot bear to hasten death again,' expressing a reluctance to die.
  • 闻者皆惨惧自悔云 - Literally 'those who heard were all filled with horror and regret,' describing the reaction of others to the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 严州 - A region in China, historically significant for its administrative divisions.
  • 观察判官 - A local government official, responsible for judicial and administrative duties.
  • 京东 - A region in China, historically significant for its geographical and political importance.
  • 冥吏 - Officials in the underworld, often depicted in Chinese folklore and literature as figures who judge the dead.
  • 徒刑 - A form of punishment involving forced labor, common in ancient Chinese legal systems.
  • 公牍 - Official documents, including legal papers and administrative records.
  • 冥冥不知人 - A state of unconsciousness or confusion, often associated with near-death experiences in Chinese literature.
  • 冥吏之戒 - Advice or warnings from officials in the underworld, often used to convey moral or spiritual lessons.

Proper Nouns

  • 严州 - A region in China, historically significant for its administrative divisions.
  • 观察判官 - A local government official, responsible for judicial and administrative duties.
  • 京东 - A region in China, historically significant for its geographical and political importance.
  • 冥吏 - Officials in the underworld, often depicted in Chinese folklore and literature as figures who judge the dead.
  • 徒刑 - A form of punishment involving forced labor, common in ancient Chinese legal systems.
  • 公牍 - Official documents, including legal papers and administrative records.
  • 冥冥不知人 - A state of unconsciousness or confusion, often associated with near-death experiences in Chinese literature.
  • 冥吏之戒 - Advice or warnings from officials in the underworld, often used to convey moral or spiritual lessons.

[1211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 誤歐 - Colloquial expression meaning 'mistakenly killed' or 'accidentally struck.'
  • 何以名为人 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how can one call oneself a person' if they cannot seek justice.
  • 具牒 - Refers to preparing a formal petition or complaint document.
  • 诣县 - Means 'to go to the county office' or 'to report to the authorities.'
  • 固与富翁善 - Means 'had a good relationship with the wealthy man.'
  • 讽使来祈 - Means 'to hint or persuade to come and plead.'
  • 薄有所觊 - Means 'to expect a small reward' or 'to hope for a minor benefit.'
  • 当谕使止之 - Means 'should be instructed to stop him.'
  • 敬听谢不敢 - Means 'to respectfully listen and apologize, saying they will not do it.'
  • 别致三百千为方君谢才数月 - Means 'to give another 300,000 coins as a gift to Mr. Fang over several months.'
  • 执送邑惩治之 - Means 'to arrest and punish him in the county.'
  • 泄 - Means 'to expose' or 'to reveal.'
  • 但用前策 - Means 'to use the previous strategy.'
  • 又如昔者之数以与仆 - Means 'to give the servant the same amount again.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淳安县 - Refers to Chun'an County, located in Zhejiang Province.
  • 严州 - Refers to Yanzhou, an ancient administrative region in Zhejiang.
  • 大姓方氏 - Refers to a prominent family named Fang.
  • 富翁 - Refers to a wealthy man.
  • 仆 - Refers to a servant.
  • 县 - Refers to a county office, the local government.
  • 邑 - Refers to a county or town.
  • 惩治 - Refers to punishment or disciplinary action.

Proper Nouns

  • 淳安县 - The name of the county.
  • 严州 - The name of the ancient administrative region.
  • 大姓方氏 - The name of the prominent family.
  • 富翁 - The name of the wealthy man.
  • 仆 - The name of the servant.
  • 县 - The name of the county office.
  • 邑 - The name of the county or town.
  • 惩治 - The term for punishment.

[1212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 適得中都一知舊訊 - Literal phrase meaning 'just received a message from an acquaintance in Zhongdu.' Sets the context for the narrative.
  • 倩市漆二百斤 - 'Qian shi qi er bai jin' means 'asked to buy two hundred catties of lacquer.' The term '倩' is a formal way of asking for something, often with a sense of favor.
  • 仓卒不办买 - 'Cangcu bu ban mai' means 'in a hurry, unable to buy.' Conveys a sense of urgency and difficulty.
  • 当辇钱以偿直 - 'Dang nian qian yi chang zhi' means 'I will send money to repay the cost.' '辇钱' (nian qian) refers to sending money, often by cart.
  • 蒙君力如许 - 'Meng jun li ru xu' means 'I have received such great help from you.' The term '蒙' (meng) implies receiving a favor.
  • 兹细事 - 'Zi xi shi' means 'this is a small matter.' The term '兹' (zi) is used to emphasize the smallness of the matter.
  • 吾家故有之 - 'Wu jia gu you zhi' means 'my family has it.' The term '故' (gu) implies that it is a long-standing possession.
  • 何用言价 - 'He yong yan jia' means 'why talk about the price.' Conveys a sense of generosity and disregard for monetary value.
  • 即如数送漆 - 'Ji ru shu song qi' means 'immediately sent the lacquer as requested.' The term '即' (ji) emphasizes the immediacy of the action.
  • 我过误杀人 - 'Wo guo wu sha ren' means 'I mistakenly killed someone.' The term '过误' (guo wu) implies a mistake or error.
  • 法不至死 - 'Fa bu zhi si' means 'the law does not warrant death.' Sets up the legal context.
  • 畏狱吏求货无艺 - 'Wei yu li qiu huo wu yi' means 'fear of prison officials who demand bribes without limit.' The term '无艺' (wu yi) means 'without limit' or 'unbounded.'
  • 将荡覆吾家 - 'Jiang dang fu wu jia' means 'will destroy my family.' The term '荡覆' (dang fu) implies a complete destruction.
  • 吾老矣 - 'Wu lao yi' means 'I am old now.' Conveys a sense of age and resignation.
  • 有死而已 - 'You si er yi' means 'there is only death left.' Conveys a sense of finality.
  • 乃距户自经 - 'Nai ju hu zi jing' means 'then he closed the door and hanged himself.' The term '距户' (ju hu) means 'closed the door,' and '自经' (zi jing) means 'hanged himself.'
  • 逾三年 - 'Yu san nian' means 'after three years.' Sets the timeline for the events.
  • 方君为鄂州浦圻宰 - 'Fang jun wei e zhou pu qi zai' means 'Fang Jun became the magistrate of Puqi in Ezhou.' The term '宰' (zai) means 'magistrate.'
  • 白昼恍恍 - 'Bai zhou huang huang' means 'in broad daylight, dazed.' The term '恍恍' (huang huang) implies a state of confusion or daze.
  • 于厅事对群吏震悸言曰 - 'Yu ting shi dui qun li zhen ji yan yue' means 'in the hall, facing many officials, he spoke in a trembling voice.' The term '震悸' (zhen ji) means 'trembling' or 'shaking.'
  • 固知翁必来 - 'Gu zhi weng bi lai' means 'I knew that the old man would come.' The term '固' (gu) implies certainty.
  • 我屡取翁钱 - 'Wo lv qu weng qian' means 'I have taken money from the old man many times.' The term '屡' (lv) means 'many times' or 'repeatedly.'
  • 而竟速翁于死 - 'Er jing su wong yu si' means 'and ultimately hastened his death.' The term '速' (su) means 'to hasten' or 'to accelerate.'
  • 翁宜此来 - 'Wong yi ci lai' means 'the old man should come here.' The term '宜' (yi) implies appropriateness or necessity.
  • 不及与妻子一语 - 'Bu ji yu qi zi yi yu' means 'without saying a word to his wife and children.' The term '不及' (bu ji) means 'not having the chance' or 'not being able to.'
  • 仆地卒 - 'Pu di cu' means 'he fell to the ground and died.' The term '仆地' (pu di) means 'fell to the ground,' and '卒' (cu) means 'died.'
  • 吏以所见白 - 'Li yi suo jian bai' means 'the official reported what he saw.' The term '白' (bai) means 'to report' or 'to inform.'
  • 始知其冥报云 - 'Shi zhi qi ming bao yun' means 'it was then known that there was a karmic retribution.' The term '冥报' (ming bao) refers to karmic retribution or divine justice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中都 - Refers to a city or region, possibly a specific place known in the context of the story.
  • 漆 - Refers to lacquer, a valuable material used for various purposes, including furniture and decoration.
  • 翁 - Refers to an elderly man, often used as a respectful term.
  • 法不至死 - Refers to the legal system of the time, indicating that the crime did not warrant the death penalty.
  • 狱吏 - Refers to prison officials or jailers, who were often corrupt and demanded bribes.
  • 鄂州浦圻 - Refers to a specific location in Hubei Province, where Fang Jun served as a magistrate.
  • 冥报 - Refers to karmic retribution or divine justice, a concept deeply rooted in Chinese religious and philosophical beliefs.

Proper Nouns

  • 中都 - A specific city or region.
  • 漆 - Lacquer, a valuable material.
  • 翁 - An elderly man.
  • 法 - The legal system.
  • 狱吏 - Prison officials.
  • 鄂州浦圻 - A specific location in Hubei Province.
  • 冥报 - Karmic retribution or divine justice.

[1213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 意绪忽昏昏不佳 - Describes a sudden, vague sense of drowsiness or unease, indicating a state of mind.
  • 枕胡床假寐 - Refers to resting on a trundle bed (胡床) or low chair, taking a short nap.
  • 大期甚不远 - Means 'the appointed time is not far off,' referring to death or a significant end.
  • 惘然如梦觉 - Describes a state of confusion or waking from a dream.
  • 挂冠 - A euphemism for resigning from office, literally 'hanging up one's hat.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 薛贺州 - Xue Hezhou is likely a historical figure, possibly a local official during the Song Dynasty (1127–1279).
  • 贺州 - A prefecture in Guangxi, China, during the Song Dynasty.
  • 朝请郎 - A low-ranking official position in the Song Dynasty, often given to those who had retired but were still consulted.
  • 祠官 - A position in the imperial temple service, often held by retired officials.
  • 越 - A region in Zhejiang, historically significant for its culture and economy.
  • 淳熙三年 - The third year of the Chunxi era (1176 CE) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 郊恩 - Refers to the imperial favor granted to officials or their families through the imperial examination system.
  • 任子 - A privilege granted to officials' children, allowing them to hold positions without passing the imperial examination.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛贺州 - Xue Hezhou, the name of the protagonist.
  • 贺州 - The prefecture where Xue served.
  • 朝请郎 - A specific official title.
  • 祠官 - A specific position in the imperial temple service.
  • 越 - A specific region in Zhejiang.
  • 淳熙三年 - A specific year during the Southern Song Dynasty.

[1214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俸入不能给妻孥 - Means 'the salary is insufficient to support his wife and children,' highlighting the financial hardship of the official.
  • 官况萧条 - Means 'the official's situation is bleak,' describing the overall poor condition of the official's life.
  • 地多塘泺 - Means 'the area is full of ponds and lakes,' indicating the natural landscape.
  • 舍蒲藕鱼鳖之外 - Means 'except for reeds, lotus roots, fish, and turtles,' listing the available food sources.
  • 市井绝无可买 - Means 'there is nothing else to buy in the market,' emphasizing the scarcity of goods.
  • 前后监司未尝至 - Means 'neither the previous nor the current supervisors have ever come here,' indicating the isolation of the place.
  • 二年憔悴在三鸦 - Means 'for two years, I have been emaciated in Sanyazi,' expressing the official's suffering.
  • 每日两餐唯是藕 - Means 'every day, my two meals consist only of lotus roots,' highlighting the simplicity of the diet.
  • 看看口里出莲花 - Means 'my mouth is filled with lotus flowers,' a poetic way of saying that the official's diet is dominated by lotus roots.
  • 平江九百一斤羊 - Means 'in Pingjiang, one pound of lamb costs nine hundred coins,' indicating the high price of lamb.
  • 俸薄如何敢买尝 - Means 'with my meager salary, how dare I buy and taste it,' expressing the official's financial constraints.
  • 只把鱼虾充两膳 - Means 'I only eat fish and shrimp for my two meals,' describing the official's simple diet.
  • 肚皮今作小池塘 - Means 'my stomach has become a small pond,' a poetic way of saying that the official's diet is limited to fish and shrimp.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三鸦镇 - A specific place name, likely referring to a town or settlement in Hebei province.
  • 汝州鲁山县 - Refers to a county in the present-day Henan province, indicating the location of another '三鸦镇.'
  • 运使 - A high-ranking official responsible for transportation and logistics.
  • 监司 - Refers to supervisory officials.
  • 林安宅居仁 - A historical figure, likely a local official or scholar.
  • 通判沈度公雅 - Another historical figure, likely a local official or scholar.
  • 高师鲁 - The narrator of the story, likely a local official or scholar.

Proper Nouns

  • 三鸦镇 - A specific place name.
  • 汝州鲁山县 - A specific place name.
  • 运使 - A specific title for a high-ranking official.
  • 监司 - A specific title for supervisory officials.
  • 林安宅居仁 - A specific historical figure.
  • 通判沈度公雅 - A specific historical figure.
  • 高师鲁 - A specific historical figure.

[1215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 垂拱而天下治赋 - Phrase from the *Book of Han* meaning 'to govern the world with ease and without much effort,' often used to describe an ideal ruler.
  • 秋风生桂枝诗 - A type of poetry associated with the autumn season and the blooming of osmanthus flowers.
  • 买舟趋郡 - To hire a boat to go to the prefectural capital, indicating travel for an official purpose.
  • 肆微言以蛊之 - To speak flatteringly in an attempt to seduce her, where '蛊' (gǔ) means to bewitch or entice.
  • 竟不第以死 - He died without passing the examination, indicating a tragic end.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴十七年 - The year 1147 during the reign of Emperor Gaozong of the Song Dynasty, a period of relative peace and cultural flourishing.
  • 京师 - The capital city, which moved to Hangzhou after the fall of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 秀州 - An administrative division in the southeastern part of China, known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 许市 - A market town within Xiu Prefecture, where the story takes place.
  • 巡检 - A local official responsible for law enforcement and public safety.
  • 淮西 - A strategic region during the Song Dynasty, often involved in military and political conflicts.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘尧举 - The name of the protagonist, a young man who travels to the prefectural capital for an examination.
  • 刘观 - The father of Liu Yaoju, a local official.
  • 秀州 - The administrative division where the story takes place.
  • 许市 - The market town within Xiu Prefecture.
  • 淮西 - The region where Liu Yaoju later serves as an official.

[1216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 得法甚的 - Meaning 'very accurate method' or 'precise technique.'
  • 被酒 - Meaning 'drunk' or 'under the influence of alcohol.'
  • 端丽绝人 - Meaning 'extremely beautiful and elegant.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夷堅丁志 - A collection of strange stories and tales from the Song Dynasty, often containing supernatural or ghostly elements.
  • 商水 - A county in Henan Province, China.
  • 严州 - A prefecture in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 笄女 - A woman who has reached the age of majority and has had her hair pinned up, symbolizing adulthood.
  • 魅 - A malevolent spirit or demon.
  • 里豪 - A village leader or influential person in the village.

Proper Nouns

  • 夷堅丁志 - Title of the text, part of *Yijian Zhi* (Record of Strange Things).
  • 丙志 - Section of *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 梁子正 - Liang Zizheng, the storyteller.
  • 路当可 - Lu Dangke, the subject of the story.
  • 商水 - Shangshui County.
  • 严州 - Yan Prefecture.
  • 方氏 - Last name of the old friend.
  • 叶氏 - Last name of the village leader.
  • 滕彦智 - Teng Yanzhi, another speaker in the story.
  • 君宝 - Junbao, Lu Dangke's father.

[1217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 默惊羡 - Silently amazed and envious, expressing a strong emotional reaction.
  • 以为向所未睹 - Thought it was something never seen before, indicating a sense of novelty and wonder.
  • 女忽奋而前 - The woman suddenly rushed forward, describing a sudden and energetic action.
  • 吾惘然变色 - I was bewildered and changed color, indicating a state of confusion and fear.
  • 急趋避于佛堂中 - Hurriedly retreated to the Buddhist hall, describing a quick and urgent action to seek refuge.
  • 从其家求閴静处 - Sought a quiet place from their house, indicating a search for a peaceful environment.
  • 将具奏于天 - Intended to present a petition to heaven, suggesting a religious or spiritual act.
  • 主人引吾至西边小圃 - The host led me to a small garden on the west side, describing a guided journey to a specific location.
  • 一堂前后皆巨竹 - A hall surrounded by tall bamboo trees, indicating a natural and serene setting.
  • 与所居相□ - Ambiguous; could mean 'in contrast to where I live' or 'in relation to where I live.' The exact meaning depends on the context.
  • 吾取箧笥朱丹符笔之属 - I took out red vermilion talismans and writing tools, indicating preparation for a ritual or writing activity.
  • 置几上 - Placed on the table, describing a simple action.
  • 未暇举笔、俄蒙然无所知 - Had no time to pick up the pen, indicating a lack of opportunity. Soon became unaware of everything, suggesting a sudden loss of consciousness or awareness.
  • 闭目审听 - Closed my eyes and listened carefully, describing a focused and attentive state.
  • 觉身在虚空 - Felt myself in虚空 (a void), indicating a sense of being in an empty or ethereal space.
  • 坐处摇兀不小定 - The place where I sat was shaking and unstable, suggesting a physical sensation of movement or instability.
  • 盖已见縻于竹抄 - It seems I was bound by bamboo, indicating a physical constraint or entanglement.
  • 食顷还故处 - After a meal, returned to the original place, indicating a passage of time and a return to a previous location.
  • 则凡桉牕户。皆粪秽狼藉不可处 - Then all the tables, windows, and doors were covered in filth and disorder, making it impossible to stay, describing a deteriorated and unclean environment.
  • 度未能与敌 - Considered it impossible to fight back, indicating a sense of helplessness or defeat.
  • 急唤仆肩舆出外 - Hurriedly called a servant to carry me out, describing a desperate action to escape.
  • 行十许里 - Traveled about ten miles, indicating a distance.
  • 适得道观 - Just happened upon a Taoist temple, suggesting a fortuitous discovery.
  • 遂托宿 - Then stayed overnight, indicating a temporary lodging arrangement.
  • 精神稍宁 - My spirit gradually calmed down, describing a state of mental relief.
  • 始趋庭中 - Then rushed into the courtyard, describing a quick and urgent action.
  • 望斗下 - Looked down at the constellation of the Big Dipper, indicating a specific direction or point of reference.
  • 焚香百拜谢过 - Burned incense and bowed a hundred times to apologize, describing a ritual act of penance.
  • 退而焚奏章 - Then burned the petition, indicating a final act of disposal.
  • 留两宿 - Stayed for two nights, indicating a duration of time.
  • 微似有影响 - Slightly seemed to have an effect, suggesting a subtle change or impact.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 佛堂 - Refers to a Buddhist hall or temple, indicating a place of worship and spiritual significance.
  • 朱丹符笔 - Refers to red vermilion talismans and writing tools, which are often used in Chinese rituals and magical practices.
  • 道观 - Refers to a Taoist temple, indicating a place of Taoist worship and practice.
  • 焚香百拜 - Refers to a ritual act of burning incense and bowing, which is common in both Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 奏章 - Refers to a petition or document presented to a deity or authority, indicating a form of communication with the divine.
  • 斗下 - Refers to the constellation of the Big Dipper, which is a significant celestial body in Chinese astronomy and folklore.

[1218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一道流 - Literally 'one Daoist,' but in this context likely refers to a Daoist practitioner or a person skilled in Daoist rituals. Could also be interpreted as a 'hermit' or 'mystic.'
  • 诣叶氏物色之 - 'Go to the Ye family to investigate/search for it.' '物色' is a classical term meaning to search or investigate.
  • 火昨从圃中堂起 - 'The fire started yesterday from the hall in the garden.' '从...起' is a common structure meaning 'to start from.'
  • 尽焫丛竹 - 'Completely burned the bamboo grove.' '焫' is an archaic term for burning.
  • 延及山后高林 - 'Spreading to the high forest behind the mountain.' '延及' means to extend to or reach.
  • 门前屋数十区 - 'Dozens of houses in front of the gate.' '区' is a unit of measure for buildings or areas.
  • 并土地小庙 - 'Including a small temple for the land god.' '土地' refers to the god of the land, a common deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 皆煨烬 - 'All reduced to ashes.' '煨烬' means charred or reduced to ash.
  • 吾知讼已直 - 'I know the lawsuit has been decided in our favor.' '讼' refers to a legal dispute or lawsuit; '直' means just or correct.
  • 自还扣之 - 'I returned to knock on it.' '扣' can mean to knock or strike, but in this context likely refers to visiting or approaching.
  • 一家长少正相贺 - 'The whole family, old and young, were joyfully celebrating.' '长少' refers to both the elderly and the young.
  • 云女经年冥冥不知人 - 'She said that the girl had been in a trance for years, unaware of people.' '冥冥' means in a trance or unaware.
